intended_use: The CADDIE computer-assisted detection device is intended to assist the gastroenterologist in detecting suspected colorectal polyps only. The gastroenterologist is responsible for reviewing CADDIE sus
intended_use_full: The CADDIE computer-assisted detection device is intended to assist the gastroenterologist in detecting suspected colorectal polyps only. The gastroenterologist is responsible for reviewing CADDIE suspected polyp areas and confirming the presence or absence of a polyp based on their own medical judgment. CADDIE is not intended to replace a full patient evaluation, nor is it intended to be relied upon to make a primary interpretation of endoscopic procedures, medical diagnosis, or recommendations of treatment/course of action for patients. The CADDIE computer-assisted detection device is limited for use with standard white-light endoscopy imaging only.
ground_truth: Histopathological confirmation for polyp detection; clinical annotation by trained clinical annotators reviewed by expert endoscopists with >2000 procedures experience for bench testing; histologically confirmed adenomas and adenocarcinomas for clinical outcomes
other_metrics: Object-level TPR: 98.27% [95% CI: 97.33, 99.20]; Frame-level FPR: 1.54% [95% CI: 1.31, 1.76]; Object-level FPR: 0.89 [95% CI: 0.79, 1.00] false alarms per minute; Frame-level TPR: 54.92% [95% CI: 53.02, 56.81]; Adenomas per colonoscopy (APC) ratio: 1.33 [95% CI: 1.06, 1.67] for CADe vs SoC (p=0.01); Cecum AI frame-level TPR: 94.05% [95% CI: 91.58, 96.52]; Cecum AI frame-level FPR: 12.08% [95% CI: 10.14, 14.02]; Cecum AI accuracy: 90.27% [95% CI: 88.73, 91.80]; Positive percent agreement (PPA): 53.9% for CADe vs 53.4% for SoC (0.5% difference)
